# Error Handling Checklist: PR Checks Workflow

**Purpose**: Validate that error handling requirements in the spec and plan are complete, clear, consistent, and cover all edge cases for CI failure reporting, actionable error messages, graceful skip behavior, and failure propagation across all workflow jobs (detect, test, validate-pr-title).
**Created**: 2026-04-01
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness: Plugin Test Failure Reporting

- [x] CHK001 - Does the spec define the required content of the CI failure message when a plugin's test suite fails, including which plugin name and which test layer(s) failed? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, FR-011, FR-018, US-1.3] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-018 now requires the test job to include the plugin name in the job display name (`test (speckit-pro)`). US-1.3 requires log output that identifies which plugin and which tests failed. The test runner (`tests/run-all.sh`) itself produces per-layer pass/fail output. Plan §Test Job Error Output confirms no additional formatting is needed at the workflow level.
- [x] CHK002 - Are requirements defined for the test job's log output format so that the PR author can locate the failing plugin without scrolling through multiple collapsed job logs? [Completeness, Spec §FR-018] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-018 requires `name: test (${{ matrix.plugin }})` on the test job, making each plugin's pass/fail status visible at a glance in the Checks UI without expanding logs.
- [x] CHK003 - Does the spec define what the error output should contain when `tests/run-all.sh` is missing from a changed plugin directory (FR-012), including the plugin name and the expected file path? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012, FR-017, Edge Cases] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-017 now requires a human-readable error message including the plugin directory name and expected file path. Plan §Test Job Error Output provides the exact message template.
- [x] CHK004 - Is the distinction between "test failure" (exit 1) and "test runner not found" (exit 2) reflected in the CI output messages, not just the exit codes? Does the spec require human-readable messages for both cases? [Clarity, Spec §FR-012, FR-017, Plan §Exit Code Semantics] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-017 requires a human-readable message for the missing test runner case (exit 2). Plan §Test Job Error Output documents that test failures (exit 1) are already actionable via the test runner's own output. Contract §test now includes an "Error Output" column mapping exit codes to message content.
- [x] CHK005 - Are requirements defined for how the GitHub Checks UI surfaces per-plugin test results (e.g., matrix job naming convention like "test (speckit-pro)") so contributors can identify which plugin failed at a glance? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011, FR-018] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-018 explicitly requires the test job name to include `${{ matrix.plugin }}`. Contract §test now includes "Display Name" field.

## Requirement Completeness: PR Title Validation Error Messages

- [x] CHK006 - Does the spec require the error message to show the actual invalid title alongside the expected format, so the contributor can see exactly what was wrong? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, FR-016, US-3] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-016 now requires the contributor's actual invalid PR title to be echoed in the error output (e.g., `Your title: "Update readme"`). Contract and plan error templates updated to include this.
- [x] CHK007 - Is the error message content for invalid PR titles specified with enough detail to satisfy SC-005 ("Contributors can self-correct invalid PR titles on the first attempt")? Does it include all three required elements: pattern, valid types, and example? [Measurability, Spec §FR-007, SC-005] -- **PASS**: FR-007 lists all three elements (pattern, types, example). FR-016 adds the actual title echo. Contract §validate-pr-title error template includes all four elements.
- [x] CHK008 - Does the error output format in the contract (§validate-pr-title Error Output Format) include the contributor's actual PR title in the error message? [Clarity, Contract §validate-pr-title, Spec §FR-016] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Contract error template now includes `Your title: "<actual PR title echoed here>"` per FR-016.
- [x] CHK009 - Are requirements defined for the success message content when a PR title passes validation, confirming the title was checked and what pattern it matched against? [Completeness, Plan §Error Message Design, Contract §validate-pr-title] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Plan §Validate-PR-Title Error Output documents the success message: `PR title is valid: $TITLE`. Contract now includes a "Success Output Format" section.

## Requirement Completeness: Git Diff Failure Handling

- [x] CHK010 - Does the spec define what happens when `git diff --name-only` fails (e.g., due to missing base SHA, corrupted git history, or network error during fetch)? Is the error message actionable? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Exit Code Semantics] -- **PASS**: Spec edge cases document that the detect job depends on `set -euo pipefail` for error propagation, and the test job's `needs: [detect]` dependency skips testing when detect fails. The git diff error message itself comes from git, which produces actionable messages (e.g., `fatal: bad revision 'origin/main...HEAD'`). Adding a custom wrapper around git's native error messages would violate KISS (Constitution Principle VI).
- [x] CHK011 - Are requirements defined for the error message when `actions/checkout` with `fetch-depth: 0` fails (e.g., repository too large, permissions error)? Does the spec go beyond "step failure propagates via set -e" to define an actionable message? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases] -- **PASS**: Spec edge cases state "The step failure propagates via `set -e` and the job fails." The `actions/checkout` action produces its own detailed error messages including HTTP status codes and error descriptions. Custom wrapping is unnecessary and would violate KISS.
- [x] CHK012 - Does the spec define what happens when `origin/${{ github.base_ref }}` does not resolve (e.g., base branch renamed or force-pushed between PR creation and CI run)? Is this distinct from CHK030 in script-safety.md? [Coverage, Edge Case] -- **PASS**: This is addressed by the combination of two platform behaviors: (a) if the base branch is deleted, GitHub closes the PR (script-safety CHK030), and (b) if the base branch is renamed, GitHub automatically updates open PRs to target the new name. Force-pushing the base branch does not change the ref name, only the SHA, and three-dot diff handles this correctly by computing the merge base.
- [x] CHK013 - Are requirements defined for the detect job to output a clear error message (not just a non-zero exit code) when the git diff command fails, so the PR author knows the failure is infrastructure-related, not code-related? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Exit Code Semantics] -- **PASS**: The git diff command produces its own descriptive error messages. The detect job failure propagates via `set -e` and the test job is skipped via `needs: [detect]`. The GitHub Actions UI shows the detect job as "failed" with git's error message in the logs. Adding a custom infrastructure-vs-code distinction would be speculative (YAGNI) since the detect job contains only infrastructure commands.

## Requirement Completeness: Graceful Skip Behavior

- [x] CHK014 - Does the spec define what message or status annotation appears in the GitHub Checks UI when the test job is skipped because no plugins changed? Is the skip reason visible to the PR author? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, FR-019, US-4] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-019 now requires the detect job to log "No plugin directories changed in this PR. Test job will be skipped." when zero plugins are found. The test job's `if:` condition causes GitHub Actions to show "skipped" status in the Checks UI, and the detect job log explains why.
- [x] CHK015 - Are requirements defined for the detect job's output message when it finds zero changed plugin directories, so the PR author understands why tests were skipped? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-019 explicitly requires the detect job to log a summary message. Contract §detect now includes a "Log Output" section with the exact messages.
- [x] CHK016 - Does the spec distinguish between "skipped because no plugins changed" and "skipped because detect job failed" in the GitHub Checks UI? Are these distinguishable by the PR author? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Edge Cases] -- **PASS**: GitHub Actions natively distinguishes these: a `needs:` dependency failure shows the dependent job as "skipped" with the cause visible via the dependency link, while a conditional skip (`if:` false) shows as "skipped" without a failure indicator. The detect job's own status (pass vs. fail) provides the distinguishing signal. No spec requirement beyond FR-006 and the edge case documentation is needed.
- [x] CHK017 - Are requirements defined for what the detect job outputs when changed files exist but none belong to plugin directories (e.g., only root-level files changed)? Does the job log explain the filtering result? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002, FR-019, US-4.1] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-019 requires the detect job to log "No plugin directories changed in this PR." when the filter produces zero results, regardless of whether files were changed outside plugin directories.

## Scenario Coverage: Error Flow Completeness

- [x] CHK018 - Does the spec define the complete set of failure modes for each job (detect, test, validate-pr-title) and their corresponding error messages? Is there a failure mode inventory? [Coverage, Plan §Exit Code Semantics, Plan §Error Message Design] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Plan §Exit Code Semantics provides a complete failure mode table for all three jobs with exit codes and meanings. Plan §Error Message Design documents the error output for each failure mode. The combination serves as the failure mode inventory.
- [x] CHK019 - Are requirements defined for what happens when multiple plugins change and some pass while others fail? Does the spec define how partial failure is surfaced in the Checks UI? [Coverage, Spec §FR-011, FR-018, US-1.2] -- **PASS**: FR-011 requires each plugin to get its own parallel job via dynamic matrix. FR-018 requires per-plugin job naming. Each matrix job has independent pass/fail status in the Checks UI, so partial failure is inherently surfaced: the passing plugin shows green and the failing one shows red.
- [x] CHK020 - Does the spec define error handling for the scenario where `jq` or other tools used in the detect job's JSON array construction are not available on the runner? [Coverage, Edge Case, Spec §Assumptions] -- **PASS**: Spec Assumptions state "The `ubuntu-latest` runner has `bash` available and no additional tooling is required." The `jq` tool is pre-installed on all GitHub-hosted Ubuntu runners (documented in GitHub's runner-images repository). Spec edge cases now document that `jq` unavailability is an infrastructure failure that surfaces as a failed workflow run. Adding explicit `jq` availability checks would violate YAGNI.
- [x] CHK021 - Are requirements defined for what happens when the detect job outputs malformed JSON (e.g., `fromJSON()` parsing fails in the test job's matrix strategy)? [Coverage, Edge Case] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Spec edge cases now document: "If `jq` is unavailable or the script produces invalid JSON, `fromJSON()` in the test job's matrix strategy will fail with a GitHub Actions parsing error. This is an infrastructure failure that surfaces as a failed workflow run, which is the correct behavior -- it cannot silently pass."

## Acceptance Criteria Quality: Actionable Error Messages

- [x] CHK022 - Is SC-005 ("Contributors can self-correct invalid PR titles on the first attempt using the error message guidance alone") measurable and testable without subjective judgment? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005] -- **PASS**: SC-005 is measurable through the acceptance scenarios in US-3, which define the exact elements (pattern, types, examples) that must appear in the error output. FR-016 adds the actual title echo. A test can objectively verify that all elements are present in the error output. The "first attempt" aspect is validated by the completeness of the guidance (all valid types, format pattern, and concrete example).
- [x] CHK023 - Does the spec define acceptance criteria for what makes a CI failure message "actionable"? Is there a measurable definition or do the requirements rely on subjective terms like "clear" and "actionable"? [Measurability, Spec §US-3, SC-003, FR-007, FR-016, FR-017] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: The spec now quantifies "actionable" through specific requirements: FR-007 (three required error elements for title validation), FR-016 (echo actual title), FR-017 (plugin name + expected path for missing test runner), FR-018 (plugin name in job title), FR-019 (skip reason logging), FR-020 (`::error::` annotations for Checks tab visibility). These replace the subjective "clear" and "actionable" with concrete, verifiable output elements.
- [x] CHK024 - Are the error message requirements in FR-007 (pattern, types, example) sufficient for SC-005, or should the spec also require showing what part of the title failed (e.g., "invalid type prefix 'update'")? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, FR-016, SC-005] -- **PASS**: FR-016 adds the actual title echo, which allows the contributor to compare their title against the expected format. Adding diagnostic parsing (e.g., "invalid type prefix 'update'") would require parsing the title into components and generating specific error messages for each failure mode (wrong type, missing colon, missing description), which adds significant complexity for marginal value. The current approach (show what you typed + show what's expected + show examples) follows the KISS principle and is the standard pattern used by widely-adopted tools like commitlint.

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK025 - Does the error output format in the workflow contract match the requirements in FR-007 and US-3? Are all three required elements (pattern, types, example) present in the contract's error template? [Consistency, Contract §validate-pr-title, Spec §FR-007, FR-016] -- **PASS**: Contract error template includes all required elements: the actual title (FR-016), expected format pattern (FR-007), valid type prefixes (FR-007), and concrete examples (FR-007). The `::error::` annotation (FR-020) is also included.
- [x] CHK026 - Is the exit code table in Plan §Exit Code Semantics consistent with the contract's exit code table? Do both documents define the same codes with the same meanings? [Consistency, Plan §Exit Code Semantics, Contract §test] -- **PASS**: Plan defines exit codes 0 (passed), 1 (test failure), 2 (runner not found) for the test job. Contract §test defines the same three codes with matching meanings. The contract now adds an "Error Output" column extending the plan's table with message details.
- [x] CHK027 - Does the spec's edge case for "detect job fails" (needs dependency skips test job) align with the plan's exit code semantics section on job dependency propagation? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases, Plan §Exit Code Semantics] -- **PASS**: Both documents state the same behavior: the test job uses `needs: [detect]`, and GitHub Actions' default is to skip dependent jobs when a `needs` job fails. The spec edge case and plan's "Job dependency propagation" paragraph are consistent.

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK028 - Does the spec define error handling for when the `edited` event fires but the title has not actually changed (e.g., PR description was edited)? Is unnecessary re-validation addressed? [Coverage, Edge Case, Spec §FR-001, Edge Cases] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Spec edge cases now document: "The `pull_request.edited` activity type fires for any PR metadata edit (title, description, labels, etc.), not just title changes. The validate-pr-title job will re-run and pass (since the title did not change). This is acceptable: the job completes in seconds and the idempotent regex check produces the same result."
- [x] CHK029 - Are requirements defined for the scenario where the workflow is manually re-run (via "Re-run all jobs") after a transient failure? Does the spec address idempotency of the error handling? [Coverage, Edge Case] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Spec edge cases now document: "All jobs are idempotent: the detect job re-reads the git diff (deterministic), the test job re-runs test suites (deterministic for the same code), and the validate-pr-title job re-checks the current title. Manual re-runs produce correct results with no side effects."
- [x] CHK030 - Does the spec define behavior when `git diff` returns file paths that no longer exist on HEAD (e.g., deleted files)? Is the directory extraction safe for deleted-file paths? [Coverage, Edge Case, Spec §FR-014, Edge Cases] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Spec edge cases now document: "Deleted files still appear in `git diff --name-only` output with their full path, and the top-level directory extraction works identically for added, modified, and deleted files. The subsequent `test -f` check validates the directory still exists and contains a plugin manifest."
- [x] CHK031 - Are requirements defined for error output when a plugin's `tests/run-all.sh` exists but is not executable (missing chmod +x)? Does the spec distinguish this from "test runner not found"? [Coverage, Edge Case, Spec §FR-012, Edge Cases] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: Spec edge cases now document: "The test job invokes the test runner with `bash tests/run-all.sh` (explicit `bash` invocation), not `./tests/run-all.sh` (which requires execute permission). Therefore, the execute bit is not required and this scenario is not a failure mode."

## Non-Functional Requirements: Error UX

- [x] CHK032 - Does the spec define requirements for error message formatting in the GitHub Actions log (e.g., use of `::error::` workflow commands to surface errors in the PR Checks summary, not just buried in logs)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-020] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-020 now requires `::error::` workflow commands in the validate-pr-title and test jobs. Plan §GitHub Actions Annotations documents the scope and annotation budget.
- [x] CHK033 - Are requirements defined for maximum acceptable delay between PR event and error feedback delivery (complementing SC-001's 5-minute target)? [Completeness, Spec §SC-001, SC-004] -- **PASS**: SC-001 defines a 5-minute target for test feedback. SC-004 defines a 1-minute target for docs-only PRs. These together cover the error feedback timing requirements. The validate-pr-title job runs in parallel with detect (FR-005) and completes in seconds, so title validation errors are delivered well within the 5-minute target.
- [x] CHK034 - Does the spec define whether error messages should use GitHub Actions `::error::` annotations to surface failures in the PR's Checks tab summary view, making errors visible without expanding job logs? [Clarity, Spec §FR-020] -- **PASS (was Gap)**: FR-020 explicitly requires `::error::` annotations for validate-pr-title and test job failures. Plan §GitHub Actions Annotations provides implementation guidance and scope limitations.

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered CHK001-CHK034 for this checklist
- `[Gap]` markers indicate missing requirements that should be addressed in spec.md or plan.md
- All gaps remediated via additions to spec.md (FR-016 through FR-020, new edge cases) and plan.md (Error Message Design section)
- Contract updated with: error output template including actual title, success output format, test job display name, detect job log output, and test job error output column
